LAW OFFICES

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

WALTER L. NOSSAMAN
(1885-1964)

LOS ANGELES
SUITE 1200
444 SOUTH FIGUEROA STREET
LOS ANGELES, CA 90071-1602
(213) 612-7800

ORANGE COUNTY
SUITE 1250
650 TOWN CENTER DRIVE
COSTA MESA, CA 92626-1981
(714) 835-9000

SUITE 1000
915 L STREET
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814-3701
TELEPHONE (916) 442-8888
FACSIMILE (916) 442-0382

WARREN ELLIOTT
OF COUNSEL

SAN FRANCISCO
THIRTY-FOURTH FLOOR
50 CALIFORNIA STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94111-4712
(415) 398-3600

WASHINGTON, D.C.
SUITE 800
1227 25TH STREET, N.W.
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20037-1156
(202) 965-6800

REFER TO FILE NUMBER

November 14, 1991

Hand-Delivered

Mr. Ben Davidian, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Operation Gobble: Charitable Event by Members of the
    California Water Association ("CWA")

Dear Mr. Davidian:

     This letter confirms the understanding reached this
morning at a meeting in your office concerning the charitable
event dubbed "Operation Gobble", sponsored by members of the
California Water Association ("CWA"). In addition to yourself,
the meeting was attended by Mr. Ordos, Mr. Hallbrain, Ms.
Wardlow, Ms. Catzen and me. Ms. Catzen and I represented CWA.
Our purpose was to receive advice and guidance regarding the
structuring of Operation Gobble for the year 1991, so that the
planned charitable contributions would not be characterized as
"political contributions" (pursuant to Gov. Code §82015 and
FPPC Regulation §18215(a)), or as "gifts" (pursuant to Gov.
Code §82028 and FPPC Regulation §18493.)

     Operation Gobble is a charitable program aimed at
providing thousands of gift turkeys to needy families
throughout California during the holiday season. In each local
area, participating private water companies have purchased
turkeys for donation to worthy charities, which distribute them
to the families.

     The program has been popular and successful, often
gaining the attention of local media and public officials.
Most importantly, CWA expects to feed more than 50,000 people

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT


Mr. Ben Davidian
November 14, 1991
RE:  Operation Gobble
Page 2


in 1991.  Legislators have often participated by recommending
specific worthy charities in their districts.  The companies
use this information to target special need.  Legislators also
may attend an event at which the turkeys are handed out.

     The Political Reform Act issue arose when it was
suggested that the Commission might construe the "publicity
value" for a participating legislator as a political
contribution.  The concern was based on the possibility that
legislators might be provided either free publicity (if their
names were used in conjunction with the event) or an exclusive
political forum.

     Because of the charitable intent of the program, you
and your staff have now provided an accommodation for 1991 so
that the turkey donation will not be construed as political
contributions or gifts.  Our understanding is that neither the
legislators nor the water companies will be required to report
the donations to the Commission so long as they adhere to the
following guidelines:

     (1)  The program shall be controlled by the donors and
          not by elected officials.  The turkeys will not
          be delivered to the legislator but will be
          delivered by the water companies directly to the
          families or the charitable organizations.

     (2)  The involvement of the legislator shall be
          limited.  Legislators may be consulted for advice
          regarding which charitable groups are most
          appropriate in their districts.  The water
          company may also coordinate scheduling of the
          charitable event with the legislator, but will
          otherwise organize the event without
          participation of the legislator.  The event will
          not be an exclusive political forum for any
          particular elected official, but shall be open to
          the public including any potential political
          opponents of the elected official.


430/JDA:jw

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT


Mr. Ben Davidian
November 14, 1991
RE: Operation Gobble
Page 3


     (3)  Publicity for the legislator shall be limited.
          Any press releases naming the elected official
          shall be sent by the legislator without
          participation by the water company. Any press
          release sent by the water company or CWA shall
          not identify a particular elected official by
          name, but may indicate that local legislators are
          expected to attend. At the event, no posters or
          signs or labels on turkeys bearing the names of
          elected officials shall be made part of the event
          by the water company. It is recognized that the
          water company cannot restrain elected officials
          or members of the public from wearing T-shirts,
          buttons, or bringing other political material,
          but the event itself will not deliberately
          feature any such publicity.

     (4)  The true source of the donation shall be
          identified. No representation will be made that
          the source of the donation is the elected
          official. On the other hand, it may be
          communicated to the charity that an elected
          official designated the charity. The donation
          itself will clearly be credited to the water
          company.

     (5)  Costs of the Event shall be limited to incidental
          expenses. No costs associated with the
          charitable event shall be reportable so long as
          the costs are for incidental matters such as
          refreshments for volunteers, press releases,
          transportation costs, or any costs necessary to
          deliver the turkeys. On the other hand, any
          significant costs aimed at creating a political
          forum for an elected official will be examined on
          a case-by-case basis. For example, if a water
          company rents a hall or buys television time to
          feature a presentation by an elected official in
          connection with the event, a political
          contribution might result.


430/JDA:jw

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX 8 ELLIOTT


Mr. Ben Davidian
November 14, 1991
RE: Operation Gobble
Page 4


According, we will advise our clients and any
participating legislators that no reporting shall be necessary
so long as these guidelines are observed.

Thank you for demonstrating good will and leadership
in this process. Please confirm this understanding in writing
at your earliest convenience.

Sincerely,

John D. Adkisson of
NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT

CC: Wayne Ordos
Scott Hallabrin
Carla Wardlow
Sharun Carlson
Meg Catzen




430/JDA:jw

LAW OFFICES

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

WALTER L. NOSSAMAN
(1886-1964)

SUITE 1000
915 L STREET
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814-3701
TELEPHONE (916) 442-6888
FACSIMILE (916) 442-0382

WARREN ELLIOTT
OF COUNSEL

LOS ANGELES
THIRTY-FIRST FLOOR
445 SOUTH FIGUEROA STREET
LOS ANGELES, CA 90071-1602
(213) 612-7800

SAN FRANCISCO
THIRD FLOOR
100 PINE STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94111-5295
(415) 543-2700

ORANGE COUNTY
SUITE 250
655 BUSINESS DRIVE
COSTA MESA, CA 92626-1981
(714) 835-9000

WASHINGTON, D.C.
SIXTH FLOOR
1140 19TH STREET, N.W.
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20036-6699
(202) 223-9100

WRITER'S DIRECT DIAL NUMBER

REFER TO FILE NUMBER


January 21, 1992

Deanne Stone, Esq.
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

RE:  Operation Gobble: Your File No. A-91-512

Dear Ms. Stone:

     Thank you for your letter dated January 17, 1992, concerning the California Water Association's "Operation Gobble."  I have reviewed your letter to Senator Presley (No. A-91-553).  Of course, I agree with your analysis of the facts as described in your advice letter.  For your information, however, we were under the impression that the birds were distributed by the company staff and that Senator Presley was not identified as the donor.  Considering the large number of participants in the program, it is not surprising that some confusion should exist.

     Although I was not personally apprised of the facts in this particular instance, please be aware that each legislator involved in the program was mailed a list of the criteria.  Whomever facts exceeding the criteria were known to this office or to our client, we advised the legislator to report the turkeys as "in-kind" political contributions.  At least a half dozen legislators were so advised.

     I am pleased that Senator Presley contacted you about the program since there was apparent confusion over the guidelines, however, I would have provided the identical guidance had his staff apprised me of the same facts.  If it

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT


Deanne Stone, Esq.
January 21, 1992
RE: Operation Gobble: Your File No. A-91-512
Page 2


would save your office time or inconvenience in the future, I
would not object to your referring such calls to me. I will
answer such questions without charge to the legislator or the
campaign committee.

     In the meantime, I assure you that pursuant to your
advice, I will re-check the facts with respect to the program
as administered by the Southern California Water Company.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        [signature]
                                        John D. Adkisson of
                                        NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT

496/JDA:jw

cc:  M. Catzen
     J. Young
     S. Carlson
